rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxyselenenylation of alkenyl alcohols with diaryl diselenides to access selenium-functionalized oxygen-containing heterocycles (benzoxepines/benzodioxepines)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (2a) to a diselenide radical cation that fragments to PhSe• and PhSe+; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical adduct (Int II) from PhSe• addition to alkene
carbocation (Int III) after anodic oxidation of C-radical
cyclized oxocarbenium/protonated ether prior to deprotonation

electron_flow: At the anode, PhSeSePh is oxidized to its radical cation and fragments to PhSe• (and PhSe+/half equiv PhSeSePh); PhSe• adds to the pendant alkene of the alkenyl alcohol to give a C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that is trapped intramolecularly by the tethered hydroxyl, followed by deprotonation; the cathode reduces H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3aa; TEMPO–SePh adduct detected by HRMS, supporting PhSe• radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3aa formed, indicating radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: PhSSPh (2f)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction — PhS• not generated under these conditions

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Diselenide 2a oxidizes at lower potential than substrate 1a, indicating 2a is the species oxidized at the anode

notes: Authors propose alkene functionalization is initiated by anodically generated PhSe• (not by direct oxidation of substrate), supported by CV showing 2a is more easily oxidized than 1a. Alternative S- and Te-based analogues fail, consistent with selectivity for Se radical generation. Water content critically influences yield, suggesting competing hydrolysis/oxidation side pathways.
